question: After the April 2026 MoneyGram/Stellar partnership extension indexed by Lumenloop, what does Scout list MoneyGram as on Stellar, and which official SEP defines its wallet-facing cash-in/cash-out flow?

golden.answer: Lumenloop indexes the 2026-04-22 MoneyGram/Stellar partnership-extension announcement and related coverage about expanding stablecoin-backed services beyond Colombia into El Salvador. As observed on 2026-07-11, Scout lists MoneyGram Access/Ramps as a live Stellar anchor that provides fiat cash on- and off-ramps for USDC; its anchor profile names both ramp directions and SEP-24. Official Stellar docs define SEP-24 as Hosted Deposit and Withdrawal, the interactive wallet-to-anchor flow in which the anchor presents the user experience and gathers the information/KYC needed for cash-in or cash-out. The announcement supplies the dated event, Scout supplies the current role/profile, and the docs supply the protocol semantics.
keyFacts: Identifies the indexed April 22, 2026 MoneyGram/Stellar partnership-extension event and its expansion of stablecoin-backed services.
Uses Scout to identify MoneyGram Access/Ramps as a live Stellar anchor offering USDC fiat on- and off-ramps.
Uses official docs to identify SEP-24 as the hosted interactive wallet-to-anchor deposit/withdrawal flow.
avoid: Do NOT call SEP-31 the wallet-facing MoneyGram cash-in/cash-out flow.
Do NOT claim the April partnership announcement alone proves the current Scout anchor profile.
golden.notes: All three service-family contributions are required; numeric location/country claims are intentionally not gated.

sources.ref: http://localhost:8787/mcp — Lumenloop + Scout + Stellar Docs probes, observed 2026-07-11

corroboration.claim: The indexed April 2026 event is the MoneyGram/Stellar partnership extension, and Scout currently lists MoneyGram as a live USDC on/off-ramp anchor.
SEP-24 defines the hosted interactive wallet-to-anchor deposit/withdrawal flow.

verified.by: P4 Lane X cross-service live composition probe
evidence: http://localhost:8787/mcp search→execute transcript summarized in evidence-matrix.md
Sibling sweep: MoneyGram/SEP-24 cases reviewed; numeric footprint omitted because live records differ over time
